name: Comprehensive Multiple Choice Dataset
description: A diverse dataset covering multiple domains for thorough RAG system evaluation

calibration.question:
What is the capital of France?
A. London
B. Berlin
C. Paris
D. Madrid

Which programming language is widely used for data science?
A. COBOL
B. Python
C. Assembly
D. FORTRAN

What is the largest planet in our solar system?
A. Earth
B. Mars
C. Jupiter
D. Saturn

Who wrote the novel 'Pride and Prejudice'?
A. Charlotte Brontë
B. Jane Austen
C. Emily Dickinson
D. Virginia Woolf

What is the chemical symbol for gold?
A. Go
B. Au
C. Ag
D. Gd

In which year did World War II end?
A. 1944
B. 1945
C. 1946
D. 1947

Which continent is Brazil located in?
A. Asia
B. Africa
C. South America
D. North America

What does HTML stand for?
A. Hypertext Markup Language
B. High-Tech Modern Language
C. Home Tool Markup Language
D. Hyperlink and Text Markup Language

Which gas makes up approximately 78% of Earth's atmosphere?
A. Oxygen
B. Carbon dioxide
C. Nitrogen
D. Argon

Who painted 'The Starry Night'?
A. Pablo Picasso
B. Vincent van Gogh
C. Claude Monet
D. Salvador Dalí

What is the square root of 144?
A. 11
B. 12
C. 13
D. 14

Which ocean is the largest?
A. Atlantic
B. Indian
C. Arctic
D. Pacific

What is the primary function of mitochondria in cells?
A. Protein synthesis
B. Energy production
C. DNA storage
D. Cell division

Which philosopher wrote 'The Republic'?
A. Aristotle
B. Socrates
C. Plato
D. Epicurus

What is the fastest land animal?
A. Lion
B. Cheetah
C. Leopard
D. Gazelle

In which city is the headquarters of the United Nations located?
A. Geneva
B. Paris
C. New York
D. London

What is the result of 7 × 8?
A. 54
B. 56
C. 58
D. 64

Which element has the atomic number 1?
A. Helium
B. Hydrogen
C. Lithium
D. Carbon

Who composed 'The Four Seasons'?
A. Bach
B. Mozart
C. Vivaldi
D. Beethoven

test.question:
What is the smallest unit of matter?
A. Molecule
B. Atom
C. Electron
D. Proton

Which language is primarily spoken in Brazil?
A. Spanish
B. English
C. Portuguese
D. French

Who painted the Mona Lisa?
A. Vincent van Gogh
B. Pablo Picasso
C. Leonardo da Vinci
D. Claude Monet

What is the primary function of the CPU in a computer?
A. Store data permanently
B. Display graphics
C. Process instructions
D. Connect to the internet

Which planet is known as the 'Red Planet'?
A. Venus
B. Mars
C. Jupiter
D. Saturn

What is the capital of Japan?
A. Tokyo
B. Osaka
C. Kyoto
D. Nagoya

Who wrote '1984'?
A. Aldous Huxley
B. George Orwell
C. Ray Bradbury
D. Kurt Vonnegut

What is the result of 144 ÷ 12?
A. 11
B. 12
C. 13
D. 14

Which programming paradigm does Python primarily support?
A. Functional only
B. Object-oriented only
C. Procedural only
D. Multi-paradigm
